description: tty-theme — Audit remediation. Fixes for P0/P1 findings from the 2026-03-15 multi-agent security, performance, bug, and UX review. Stories ordered by dependency: security foundations first, then performance, then bugs, then UX.

userStories.title: Fix SSRF guard: add missing blocklist ranges and IPv6 mapped addresses
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I need the SSRF guard to block 0.0.0.0/8, 100.64.0.0/10, and IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses so attackers cannot bypass it with edge-case addresses.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 0.0.0.0/8 and 100.64.0.0/10 to _BLOCKED_NETWORKS in security/ssrf_guard.py
For each resolved IP, check ipaddress.ip_address(ip).ipv4_mapped and re-validate the embedded IPv4 address if present
check_url('https://0.0.0.0/') raises ValueError
check_url('https://[::ffff:127.0.0.1]/') raises ValueError
check_url('https://100.64.1.1/') raises ValueError
Existing tests still pass
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: SEC-C2 from audit. See security/ssrf_guard.py lines 9-18.

userStories.title: Fix SSRF guard: resolve DNS once and pass resolved IP to httpx (DNS rebinding fix)
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I need DNS resolution to happen once and the resolved IP to be reused for the HTTP fetch so a DNS rebinding attack cannot bypass the SSRF guard between the check and the request.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a function safe_fetch(url, **kwargs) in security/ssrf_guard.py that: (1) calls check_url(url), (2) resolves the hostname via socket.getaddrinfo, (3) validates the resolved IP, (4) performs the httpx.get with the resolved IP via a custom transport or by substituting the IP in the URL and passing the original host as the Host header
image/loader.py imports and uses safe_fetch instead of httpx.get for remote image URLs
A mock test verifies safe_fetch rejects a URL whose DNS resolves to 127.0.0.1
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: SEC-C1 from audit. DNS TOCTOU between ssrf_guard.py check and loader.py httpx.get.

userStories.title: Fix path traversal in --install theme name sanitization
userStories.description: As a user, I need the --install flag to safely handle any theme name so that a malicious name like ../../.bashrc cannot write outside the themes directory.
acceptanceCriteria: In cli/main.py _install_theme(), after building safe_name, validate it with re.match(r'^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9_-]*$', safe_name) and raise ValueError if it does not match
Names containing '/' or '..' or starting with '-' are rejected with a clear error message
tty-theme generate --prompt x --install --name '../../evil' prints an error and exits non-zero
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Restrict /metrics endpoint to internal access only
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I need the /metrics endpoint to be inaccessible to public internet clients so that internal observability data is not exposed.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a MetricsAuthMiddleware or a dependency in api/main.py that checks for a METRICS_TOKEN env var
If METRICS_TOKEN is set, /metrics requires Authorization: Bearer <token> header and returns 403 if missing or wrong
If METRICS_TOKEN is not set (local dev), /metrics is accessible without auth
.env.example documents METRICS_TOKEN= as an optional field
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix X-Forwarded-For spoofing in rate limiter
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I need the rate limiter to only trust X-Forwarded-For when behind a known proxy so that clients cannot bypass rate limits by spoofing the header.
acceptanceCriteria: In api/middleware.py _ip_hash(), add a TRUSTED_PROXY_COUNT env var (default 0, integer)
If TRUSTED_PROXY_COUNT == 0, use request.client.host directly, ignoring X-Forwarded-For
If TRUSTED_PROXY_COUNT > 0, parse the Nth-from-right value from X-Forwarded-For (the trusted hop)
.env.example documents TRUSTED_PROXY_COUNT=1 for Cloud Run behind Google Front End
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Bound rate limiter memory with TTL-based eviction
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I need the in-process rate limit dictionaries to evict stale entries so that an attacker cannot cause OOM by rotating spoofed IPs.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace _minute_buckets and _hour_buckets defaultdicts in api/middleware.py with cachetools.TTLCache(maxsize=10000, ttl=3600)
Add cachetools to pyproject.toml dependencies
After eviction, a previously seen IP gets a fresh bucket (correct behavior)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: SEC-H3 and PERF-M7 from audit. api/middleware.py lines 34-35.

userStories.title: Fix sanitize_prompt to raise ValueError on empty result and strip control characters
userStories.description: As a developer, I need sanitize_prompt to fulfil its documented contract (raise ValueError on empty) and strip ASCII control characters to prevent log injection.
acceptanceCriteria: At the end of sanitize_prompt() in security/input_sanitizer.py, add: if not normalized: raise ValueError('Prompt is empty after sanitization')
Strip all characters with Unicode category Cc and Cf (except space/tab/newline) using unicodedata.category
sanitize_prompt('') raises ValueError
sanitize_prompt('\x00\x01hello') returns 'hello'
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Validate secret name before Secret Manager path construction
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I need get_secret() to validate the secret name against an allowlist so that path injection into the Secret Manager resource path is impossible.
acceptanceCriteria: In security/secrets.py get_secret(), before constructing the resource path, validate name matches r'^[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*$'
Raise ValueError('Invalid secret name: {name}') if it does not match
get_secret('../../bad') raises ValueError
get_secret('GEMINI_API_KEY') works normally
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix sync blocking in async FastAPI generate endpoint
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I need the /v1/generate endpoint to not block the asyncio event loop so that the API can handle multiple concurrent requests.
acceptanceCriteria: Change async def generate(...) to def generate(...) in api/main.py so FastAPI delegates it to a thread pool
Remove any await calls that are no longer needed after this change
The endpoint still returns the correct GenerateResponse
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PERF-H1 from audit. api/main.py lines 114-158. Simplest fix: sync def, FastAPI auto-threads.

userStories.title: Add HTTP connection reuse to OpenAICompatProvider
userStories.description: As a developer, I need each OpenAICompatProvider instance to reuse an httpx.Client so that TCP+TLS connection overhead is not paid on every LLM call.
acceptanceCriteria: In providers/openai_compat.py OpenAICompatProvider.__init__(), create self._client = httpx.Client(timeout=60.0) and store it
Replace the standalone httpx.post() call in generate() with self._client.post()
Replace the standalone httpx.get() call in health_check() with self._client.get() with timeout=2.0
Add __del__ or a close() method that calls self._client.close()
Existing provider tests still pass
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pre-load MiniLM embedding model at FastAPI startup
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I need the sentence-transformers model to be loaded during app startup rather than on the first request so that cold start latency does not affect real users.
acceptanceCriteria: In api/main.py lifespan startup block, call cache.embeddings._get_model() (or equivalent) to force model load
The model is stored on app.state so it is reused across requests
If sentence-transformers is not installed, startup logs a warning but does not fail
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Cache provider health check results with 30-second TTL
userStories.description: As a developer, I need provider health checks to be cached for 30 seconds so that repeated calls do not add 6 seconds of serial blocking when local providers are offline.
acceptanceCriteria: In providers/openai_compat.py, add a _health_cache: dict[str, tuple[bool, float]] at module level
health_check() returns cached result if last check was within 30 seconds (use time.monotonic())
Otherwise performs the actual check and caches the result
A test verifies the second health_check() call within 30s does not call httpx.get again
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PERF-M2 from audit. providers/registry.py lines 49-50, openai_compat.py line 57.

userStories.title: Fix cache hits returning wrong serialization format (BUG-01)
userStories.description: As a user, I need --target iterm2 to always return iTerm2 format even when served from cache so that I get the format I asked for regardless of cache tier.
acceptanceCriteria: In modes/prompt_mode.py, on the tier-1 cache hit path (line ~61), re-serialize cached['theme_data'] through the serializer: parse with validator, serialize with serializer.serialize()
On the tier-2 similarity hit path (line ~73), apply the same re-serialization
A test verifies: seed cache with Ghostty format, call generate_from_prompt with target='iterm2', assert output contains XML plist markers
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: BUG-01 from audit. Critical. modes/prompt_mode.py lines 61 and 73.

userStories.title: Fix wrong prompt builder used for image refinement (BUG-06)
userStories.description: As a user, I need image refinement to use the correct LLM system prompt so that the palette-specific refine prompt is used instead of the text-inspiration prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: In modes/image_mode.py line ~73, replace build_prompt(f'Refine this extracted palette:\n{palette_str}') with build_refine_prompt(colors, user_description='') where colors is the extracted palette list
Import build_refine_prompt from generator.prompt
A test verifies the refine path calls generate with a prompt dict whose system key contains the refine system prompt text
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix image loader: tighten magic bytes and handle UnidentifiedImageError (BUG-03, BUG-14)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the image loader to correctly validate WebP magic bytes and catch PIL's UnidentifiedImageError as ValueError so the API never leaks unhandled exceptions.
acceptanceCriteria: In image/loader.py, change the RIFF entry to validate bytes 8-11 == b'WEBP': check first 12 bytes, require [0:4] == b'RIFF' and [8:12] == b'WEBP'
Remove the b'\x00\x00\x00' catch-all entry from _ALLOWED_MAGIC
Wrap the PIL Image.open() call in a try/except that catches PIL.UnidentifiedImageError and re-raises as ValueError('Unsupported or corrupt image format')
A test verifies a WAV file (RIFF container) is rejected
A test verifies an unknown binary file starting with null bytes is rejected
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix log_cost TOCTOU race with SQLite UPSERT (BUG-09)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need log_cost to use an atomic UPSERT so concurrent requests cannot double-count costs.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a UNIQUE(date, provider) constraint to the cost_log table in cache/db.py init_db()

Replace the SELECT + conditional INSERT/UPDATE in log_cost() with a single INSERT INTO cost_log(date, provider, calls, cost_usd) VALUES(?,?,1,?) ON CONFLICT(date, provider) DO UPDATE SET calls = calls + 1, cost_usd = cost_usd + excluded.cost_usd
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix tier_used always hardcoded in API response (BUG-08)
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I need the tier_used field in GenerateResponse to reflect the actual cache tier used so I can understand whether a result was cached or generated.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify generate_from_prompt() in modes/prompt_mode.py to return a tuple (theme_str, tier: int) where tier=1 for hash hit, tier=2 for similarity hit, tier=3 for LLM generation
Modify generate_from_image() in modes/image_mode.py to return (theme_str, tier: int) where tier=1 for pHash cache hit, tier=3 for fresh extraction
Update api/main.py /v1/generate to unpack the tuple and set tier_used correctly
Update cli/main.py to handle the new return value (unpack tuple, use theme_str only)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: BUG-08 from audit. api/main.py lines 135 and 145.

userStories.title: Add progress feedback to CLI generate command (CLI-3)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see progress messages during theme generation so I know the tool is working and not hung during LLM calls.
acceptanceCriteria: In cli/main.py generate(), before calling generate_from_prompt or generate_from_image, print 'searching cache...' to stderr

After provider resolution, print '  generating via <provider.name>...' to stderr (requires provider name to be accessible at that level, or print it from inside the mode functions using a callback/echo param)
All status messages go to stderr (typer.echo(..., err=True)) so they do not contaminate piped output
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CLI-3 from UX audit. cli/main.py lines 63-69.

userStories.title: Improve CLI error messages with actionable guidance (CLI-1, CLI-2)
userStories.description: As a user, I want error messages to tell me what went wrong and what to do next so I am not left with a cryptic Python exception string.
acceptanceCriteria: In cli/main.py generate(), when neither --prompt nor --image is provided, print a multi-line help message: 'Error: at least one input is required.' followed by two usage examples and 'Run tty-theme generate --help for all options.'
Catch RuntimeError from resolve_provider separately and print: 'No LLM provider available. Run tty-theme config setup to add an API key, or start Ollama with: ollama serve'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CLI-1 and CLI-2 from UX audit.

userStories.title: Add --install overwrite guard and post-install activation hint (CLI-4)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to be warned before overwriting an existing theme and told how to activate it in my terminal config after installing.
acceptanceCriteria: In cli/main.py _install_theme(), check if dest already exists before writing
If it exists, print a warning 'Theme <name> already exists at <path>. Use --force to overwrite.' and return None without writing
Add --force / -f bool flag to the generate command; if --force is passed, write without checking
After a successful Ghostty install, print to stderr: 'To activate: add `theme = <name>` to ~/.config/ghostty/config'
After a successful iTerm2 install, print to stderr: 'To activate: open iTerm2 → Preferences → Profiles → Colors → Color Presets → <name>'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CLI-4 from UX audit. cli/main.py lines 77-82.

userStories.title: Move sentence-transformers to optional dependency group
userStories.description: As a developer, I need sentence-transformers (and its 2GB PyTorch dep) to be optional so users who don't need tier-2 similarity caching don't pay the install cost.
acceptanceCriteria: Move sentence-transformers from [project.dependencies] to [project.optional-dependencies] under key 'embeddings' in pyproject.toml
In cache/embeddings.py, wrap the import in try/except ImportError and raise a clear RuntimeError('Install tty-theme[embeddings] to enable similarity search') if not available
In modes/prompt_mode.py, the ImportError guard for sentence-transformers already exists — verify it correctly skips tier-2 and falls through to tier-3
uv sync installs without sentence-transformers by default
uv sync --extra embeddings installs it
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Remove dead tomli dependency and fix GhosttySerializer file_extension
userStories.description: As a developer, I need dead code removed and the Ghostty serializer's file_extension to match the actual install path so future use of the method doesn't introduce a regression.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove the tomli conditional dependency from pyproject.toml (unreachable since requires-python>=3.11)
Change GhosttySerializer.file_extension() in generator/serializers/ghostty.py to return '' (empty string, matching the install logic in cli/main.py)
uv sync completes without error
Typecheck passes
